concept: Graceful / Moving with Elegance / Beautifully Fluid
description: Having or showing grace, elegance, and beauty of movement or form. 'Luwae' names the quality of beautiful flow—movement that delights the eye, form that pleases without effort, the natural elegance of living things.
sound_symbolism: The word flows with natural elegance. 'Lu' begins with liquid ease, movement initiated. Then 'wa' flows onward, the graceful continuation. Finally 'e' releases into completion, the movement finished beautifully.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'luwae phero' (graceful fern). Related to 'phiro' (elegant) but emphasizes movement and flow rather than refined form.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Grace was Art Nouveau's guiding principle—the flowing line, the organic curve, beauty in natural movement.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Graceful action arises from presence—when fully aware, movement becomes naturally elegant.
